$comment: Schema evolution policy: see docs/schema-evolution.md. v1 payloads are frozen; adding required fields to existing payloads requires schema_version v2.
title: VibeWarden Structured Event v1
description: Schema for all VibeWarden structured log events. Every security-relevant action emits an event conforming to this schema. Designed to be AI-readable: an LLM can understand each event from the ai_summary field alone, and can parse the payload for deeper analysis. STABILITY: v1 payloads are frozen; new fields require schema_version v2.

schema_version.description: The schema version. Consumers must reject events with unknown versions.

event_type.description: Stable identifier for the kind of event. Consumers use this to dispatch to type-specific logic. The value follows the dot-separated namespace convention (e.g. 'auth.success'). Unknown event types must be accepted and passed through — the schema is intentionally open to allow forward-compatible addition of new event types without breaking existing consumers.

timestamp.description: RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC when the event occurred.

severity.description: Severity level for agent triage. Ranges from 'info' (routine operational events) to 'critical' (events requiring immediate response).

category.description: Event category for grouping and filtering. Maps to the security domain the event belongs to.

ai_summary.description: A concise, human- and AI-readable sentence describing what happened. Includes the most relevant identifiers so an LLM can reason about the event without reading the payload.

payload.description: Event-specific structured data. The shape depends on event_type; see the conditional schemas below.

actor.description: The entity that initiated the action. Omitted for events where the actor is implicit (e.g. system-internal events with no inbound request).

resource.description: The target of the action. Omitted for informational events with no specific target resource.

outcome.description: The enforcement result of this event.

risk_signals.description: Machine-detectable risk indicators associated with this event. Omitted or empty when no risk signals were detected.

request_id.description: The inbound request identifier propagated from the client (e.g. the X-Request-ID header value). Omitted when not present.

trace_id.description: The W3C trace-id of the active OpenTelemetry span at the time the event was emitted. Omitted when no tracing context is available.

span_id.description: The W3C span-id of the active OpenTelemetry span at the time the event was emitted. Omitted when no tracing context is available.

triggered_by.description: The internal component or subsystem that raised this event (e.g. 'auth_middleware', 'rate_limit_middleware'). Omitted when the source is implicit from the event_type.

Actor.description: The entity that initiated a security-relevant action.

id.description: The actor's unique identifier within its type namespace.

ip.description: The client IP address. Present for 'ip' type actors and optionally for 'user' and 'api_key' actors when the source IP is known.

Resource.description: The target of a security-relevant action.

path.description: The URL path, route name, or file path of the resource.

method.description: The HTTP method. Only meaningful when type is 'http_endpoint'.

signal.description: Stable identifier for the risk pattern (e.g. 'brute_force', 'sqli_attempt', 'prompt_injection').

score.description: Normalised risk score in [0.0, 1.0]. Higher values indicate higher confidence.

details.description: Human-readable explanation of why this signal was raised.

ProxyStartedPayload.description: Payload for proxy.started events. Emitted once at startup when the reverse proxy is ready to accept connections.

listen.description: The address the reverse proxy is listening on (e.g. ":8080", ":8443").

upstream.description: The address requests are forwarded to (e.g. "localhost:3000").

tls_provider.description: The TLS certificate provider (e.g. "letsencrypt", "self-signed", "external"). Empty string when tls_enabled is false.

security_headers_enabled.description: Whether the security headers middleware is active.

ProxyKratosFlowPayload.description: Payload for proxy.kratos_flow events. Emitted for every request routed to the Ory Kratos self-service flow API.

method.description: The HTTP method of the request (e.g. "GET", "POST").

path.description: The URL path of the request.

AuthSuccessPayload.description: Payload for auth.success events. Emitted when a request carries a valid session and is allowed to proceed to the upstream application.

method.description: The HTTP method of the authenticated request (e.g. "GET").

path.description: The URL path of the authenticated request.

email.description: The email address associated with the authenticated identity.

AuthFailedPayload.description: Payload for auth.failed events. Emitted when a request is rejected due to a missing, invalid, or expired session.

method.description: The HTTP method of the rejected request (e.g. "GET").

path.description: The URL path of the rejected request.

reason.description: A short description of why the request was rejected (e.g. "missing session cookie", "invalid or missing session").

detail.description: Optional additional detail (e.g. an error message). May be an empty string.

RateLimitHitPayload.description: Payload for rate_limit.hit events. Emitted when a request is rejected because the caller exceeded their per-IP or per-user rate limit.

limit_type.description: The kind of limit that was exceeded.

identifier.description: The value that was rate-limited: the client IP address for limit_type "ip", or the user ID for limit_type "user".

requests_per_second.description: The configured rate limit in tokens per second.

retry_after_seconds.description: How long the caller must wait before retrying, in seconds.

path.description: The URL path of the rate-limited request.

method.description: The HTTP method of the rate-limited request.

client_ip.description: The client IP address. Present only when limit_type is "user" and the IP could be determined.

RateLimitUnidentifiedClientPayload.description: Payload for rate_limit.unidentified_client events. Emitted when a request is rejected because the client IP address could not be determined.

path.description: The URL path of the rejected request.

method.description: The HTTP method of the rejected request.

RequestBlockedPayload.description: Payload for request.blocked events. Emitted when a request is blocked by a middleware layer for a reason other than auth or rate limiting (e.g. a security policy violation).

method.description: The HTTP method of the blocked request.

path.description: The URL path of the blocked request.

reason.description: A short description of why the request was blocked.

blocked_by.description: Identifies the middleware or policy that blocked the request (e.g. "security_headers", "ip_blocklist").

client_ip.description: The client IP address. May be an empty string if the IP could not be determined.

TLSCertificateIssuedPayload.description: Payload for tls.certificate_issued events. Emitted when a new TLS certificate is successfully obtained or renewed.

domain.description: The domain name for which the certificate was issued.

expires_at.description: The certificate expiry time in RFC 3339 format.

UserCreatedPayload.description: Payload for user.created events. Emitted when a new user identity is created in the identity provider.

identity_id.description: The identity provider identifier for the new user.

email.description: The email address of the new user.

UserDeletedPayload.description: Payload for user.deleted events. Emitted when a user identity is deleted from the identity provider.

identity_id.description: The identity provider identifier of the deleted user.

email.description: The email address of the deleted user.
